title: Aureline review-anchor (anchored comment / suggestion / evidence pointer) vocabulary
description: Boundary schema for the anchored-comment record family frozen in docs/vcs/review_workspace_contract.md. One review_anchor_record pins which review surface is rendering one comment / suggestion / evidence pointer, which artifact class it anchors against (cited from schemas/review/review_surface_record.schema.json — never re-minted here), which target_kind it pins (text line range, cell-aware notebook cell, structure-aware config node, image region, evidence section, whole artifact, or commit / revision), the anchor's drift state from the closed six-value vocabulary (anchor_bound_exact, anchor_remapped_with_recorded_mapping, anchor_drifted_user_must_resolve, anchor_target_deleted_re_anchor_or_resolve, anchor_scope_unavailable, anchor_archived_tombstone), the matching required user action paired through an allOf gate so a downstream surface that re-renders a drifted or deleted anchor as bound denies with anchor_drift_state_required_action_mismatch and so a refactor that silently retargets denies with silent_anchor_relocation_forbidden, the local-vs-provider freshness chip from the closed five-value vocabulary so a reviewer can read the cue without opening the workspace, the comment-payload redaction class plus the reviewable label registry ref (the raw comment body never crosses this boundary), the posting-actor / posted-at attribution, and the audit-event vocabulary the anchor publishes. Raw absolute paths, raw branch / commit URLs, raw author identity strings, raw comment bodies, raw provider rule bodies, raw approval-ticket bodies, raw notebook cell text, raw terminal bytes, and raw URLs MUST NOT appear on any record emitted against this schema. The eventual review-anchor crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary every non-owning surface reads. Adding a new record kind, anchor-target kind, drift state, required-action value, freshness class, denial reason, or audit-event id is additive-minor and bumps review_anchor_schema_version; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

review_anchor_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the review-anchor vocabulary. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles. Raw absolute paths, raw branch / commit URLs, raw author identity strings, raw comment bodies, raw notebook cell text, raw terminal bytes, and raw URLs never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

reviewable_label.description: Redaction-aware reviewable label (<= 1024 graphemes). Carries a presentation-only summary for support / audit; never carries the raw payload. Exporters MUST apply broker-owned redaction (ADR-0007) before the value crosses this boundary.

redaction_class.description: Re-exported verbatim from schemas/governance/capability_lifecycle.schema.json (ADR-0011). Comments quoted by support exports MUST raise to internal_support_restricted; anchors that touched a credentialed flow MUST raise to operator_only_restricted.

policy_context.description: Policy context the anchor was minted under.

review_artifact_class.description: Re-exported from schemas/review/review_surface_record.schema.json with the line_oriented_source_artifact value added so anchors on plain source files (the line-oriented default not enumerated by the structured-artifact matrix) have a stable token. Adding a new value here MUST be reflected in the structured-artifact matrix in the same change.

review_surface_class.description: Re-exported from schemas/review/review_surface_record.schema.json with the line_oriented_compare_viewer value added to match the line_oriented_source_artifact addition above. The structured-artifact matrix owns the per-class default surface; this row carries the rendered-against value the anchor was posted on.

anchor_target_kind.description: Frozen anchor-target-kind vocabulary. text_line_range_anchor pairs with line-oriented or generated-source artifacts; cell_aware_notebook_anchor pairs with jupyter_notebook and carries a stable cell id ref; structure_aware_config_node_anchor pairs with structured_config_* and carries a key path or section ref; image_region_anchor pairs with image_or_design_snapshot; evidence_section_anchor pairs with evidence_packet (read-only); whole_artifact_anchor binds to the artifact as a whole; commit_or_revision_anchor binds to a commit / revision identity rather than to an artifact.

anchor_drift_state.description: Frozen anchor-drift state vocabulary. anchor_bound_exact = the target resolved exactly under the current build identity; anchor_remapped_with_recorded_mapping = the resolver followed an authoritative refactor / move with a recorded mapping (the anchor MUST cite a non-empty remap_chain_target_id_refs); anchor_drifted_user_must_resolve = the target moved but the resolver could not pick a single successor and the user MUST disambiguate; anchor_target_deleted_re_anchor_or_resolve = the target no longer exists and re-binding is forbidden without explicit user action; anchor_scope_unavailable = the workspace / sparse slice / remote shard the target lives in is currently outside admitted scope; anchor_archived_tombstone = the anchor was retired by the user or by a managed sweep and is kept as a tombstone for audit / restore. Re-running an anchor whose state is anchor_drifted_user_must_resolve, anchor_target_deleted_re_anchor_or_resolve, or anchor_scope_unavailable MUST NOT silently jump; the surface advertises the typed required user action and refuses the implicit jump (silent_anchor_relocation_forbidden).

anchor_drift_required_user_action.description: Frozen required-user-action vocabulary, paired through allOf with anchor_drift_state. A row whose pair is mismatched denies with anchor_drift_state_required_action_mismatch.

local_vs_provider_freshness_class.description: Frozen local-vs-provider freshness class, mirrored from the workspace's provider_overlay_freshness_class but specialised per anchor so the chip can read mechanically. local_only_no_provider_overlay = workspace has no overlay; local_and_provider_match_fresh = local and overlay agree and overlay is fresh; local_and_provider_match_stale_within_grace = agree but overlay is stale-within-grace; local_and_provider_disagree_user_review_required = local and provider overlay disagree on the anchor and the user MUST resolve before sending mutations through; provider_overlay_unavailable_local_continues = overlay is unavailable and the anchor cannot claim parity until the overlay returns.

review_anchor_denial_reason.description: Frozen denial-reason vocabulary on the review_anchor audit stream.

review_anchor_audit_event_id.description: Frozen audit-event ids on the review_anchor stream. review_anchor_audit_denial_emitted MUST cite a review_anchor_denial_reason.

review_workspace_id_ref.description: Opaque ref to the review_workspace_record (schemas/vcs/review_workspace.schema.json) the anchor is posted in.

target_ref.description: Opaque ref to the target the anchor pins (line range row in the line-oriented diff store, cell row in the notebook owner, key-path row in the structured-config owner, region row in the image owner, section row in the evidence-packet owner, artifact row, commit row). Raw bodies never cross this boundary; the owner contract resolves the ref.

secondary_target_ref.description: Optional secondary target ref for compound anchors (e.g. an image region tied to a paired diff hunk; a notebook cell tied to a paired exported-script line range).

remap_chain_target_id_refs.description: Ordered remap chain naming the resolver's mapping. Required non-empty when anchor_drift_state is anchor_remapped_with_recorded_mapping; MUST be empty for every other state.

supersedes_review_anchor_id_ref.description: Optional opaque ref to a prior review_anchor_record this row supersedes (used when an anchor is re-posted under a new target after the user resolved a drift).

comment_payload_label_opaque_ref.description: Opaque ref into the redaction-aware reviewable-label registry where the comment's display label resolves. The raw comment body does NOT live on this record; resolution honours comment_payload_redaction_class.

posted_actor_ref.description: Opaque ref to the posting identity. Required (non-null); a missing ref denies with review_anchor_record_required_field_missing.

archived_at.description: Required (non-null) when anchor_drift_state is anchor_archived_tombstone. Null otherwise.

tombstoned_at.description: Optional tombstone timestamp distinct from archived_at; used when the anchor is tombstoned for retention sweep but not yet archived.

hosted_review_inbox_record_id_ref.description: Forward-dependency slot reserved for the hosted-review inbox contract. Currently always null.

allOf.description: anchor_remapped_with_recorded_mapping MUST pair with no_user_action_required_anchor_bound_or_remapped and MUST cite a non-empty remap_chain_target_id_refs.
anchor_archived_tombstone MUST pair with user_must_restore_from_archive_or_acknowledge_tombstone and MUST cite archived_at.
remap_chain_target_id_refs MUST be empty when anchor_drift_state is not anchor_remapped_with_recorded_mapping.
cell_aware_notebook_anchor MUST pair with review_artifact_class = jupyter_notebook.
structure_aware_config_node_anchor MUST pair with one of the structured_config_* artifact classes.
image_region_anchor MUST pair with review_artifact_class = image_or_design_snapshot.
evidence_section_anchor MUST pair with review_artifact_class = evidence_packet.

review_anchor_record.description: Row shape consumed by review surfaces, hosted-review reader, AI review-aware overlays, and support / export. Exactly one row per anchored comment / suggestion / evidence pointer.

denial_reason.description: Required (non-null) when review_anchor_audit_event_id_value is review_anchor_audit_denial_emitted. Null otherwise.

allOf.description: denial_reason is required (non-null) when the event id is review_anchor_audit_denial_emitted and MUST be null otherwise.

review_anchor_audit_event_record.description: Audit-event row on the review_anchor stream.
